thread.title: Donald Trump names Oregon US Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer as labor secretary nominee
thread.title_full: Donald Trump names Oregon US Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer as labor secretary nominee

author: , The Register-Guard
title: Donald Trump names Oregon US Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer as labor secretary nominee
text:
Donald Trump names Oregon US Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer as labor secretary nominee
President-elect Donald Trump on Friday announced U.S. Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer, R-Oregon, as his pick for U.S. secretary of labor.
Chavez-DeRemer [narrowly lost her reelection bid](https://www.statesmanjournal.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/11/07/who-won-the-race-for-oregons-5th-congressional-district/76093858007/) for Oregon's 5th Congressional District earlier this month against state Rep. Janelle Bynum, D-Happy Valley.
Chavez-DeRemer has represented the 5th District, which covers Linn, Clackamas and Deschutes counties and parts of Multnomah and Marion counties, since 2022.
She was one of the first Latinas and the first female Republican elected to represent Oregon in Congress. Before serving in Congress, Chavez-DeRemer was elected mayor of Happy Valley in 2010 and 2014.
Her endorsements for the 2024 election included the Oregon Farm Bureau and 20 labor unions.
"Lori has worked tirelessly with both Business and Labor to build America's workforce, and support the hardworking men and women of America," Trump wrote in a prepared statement. "I look forward to working with her to create tremendous opportunity for American Workers, to expand Training and Apprenticeships, to grow wages and improve working conditions, to bring back our Manufacturing jobs."
Chavez-DeRemer did not immediately respond to a request for comment but celebrated her nomination on social media, thanking Trump.
"It's time to bring our economy to new heights and secure a prosperous future for all hardworking Americans," [she said on X](https://x.com/LChavezDeRemer/status/1860118647051813300).
If her nomination is confirmed, Chavez-DeRemer would be charged with leading the Labor Department and its more than 15,700-person workforce and $13.9 billion budget.
Dianne Lugo covers the Oregon Legislature and equity issues. Reach her at[ dlugo@statesmanjournal.com](mailto:dlugo@statesmanjournal.com) or on X [@DianneLugo](https://twitter.com/DianneLugo)
